front: Short Story: Dmitri Gurov begins an affair with Anna Sergeyevna in Yalta; they reunite at a performance of The Geisha in her hometown
Short Story: ends with the realization that "the most complicated and difficult part was only just beginning"
Short Story: a man cries eating fruit from his own bushes; the narrator says "someone with a little hammer" should remind happy people of unhappy ones
work: The Little Trilogy (Gooseberries / The Man in the Case / About Love)

front: Short Story: Byelikov, a teacher of Greek who hides from the world, is mocked by a cartoon captioned "Anthropos in love" and breaks off relations with Varinka
work: The Little Trilogy (Gooseberries / The Man in the Case / About Love)

front: Short Story: a lawyer agrees to 15 years of imprisonment for two million rubles; a gun is fired to celebrate mastery of six languages; he renounces the money and leaves early
Short Story: Dr. Rabin is locked in his own mental asylum after conversing with patient Gromov about Stoicism; has a dying vision of running deer
Play: Madame Ranevskaya sells her estate to the former serf Lopakhin; a mysterious breaking string is heard; Firs is locked in and says "They've forgotten about me"
Play: the only play by this author where a gun is shown but never fired; ends with the sound of axes chopping down the title trees
Play: Konstantin Treplev writes an experimental symbolist play featuring "all is cold, void, and terrible"; kills the title bird and later shoots himself
Play: Trigorin receives a medallion inscribed "If you ever need my life, come and take it" referencing page 121 of his novel
Play: Vanya tries to shoot Professor Serebryakov but misses in Act III; steals morphine; exclaims he could have been Schopenhauer or Dostoevsky
Play: Baron Tuzenbach is killed in a duel with Soleni over Irina's hand; Chebutykin sings "Ta-ra-ra-boom-di-ay" as Olga says "if only we knew"
Short Story: coffin-maker "Bronze" plays in a klezmer orchestra; overcomes antisemitism after wife Marfa dies; the title Jew inherits the violin
Short Story: the title phantom monk tells Korvin he is a genius; appears in a whirl of smoke; Korvin marries a horticulturalist's daughter
Short Story: a clerical apprentice tells widows about Peter's denial of Christ on Good Friday; Vasilisa is moved to tears
Short Story: based on a real burial at sea during a journey to Sakhalin; praised by Virginia Woolf in "Modern Fiction"
Playwright: his dramatic principle states that if a gun is shown in Act I, it must be fired by the end of the play
Playwright: Raymond Carver (the "American Chekhov") depicted this author bleeding from his mouth and dying in the story "Errand"
